content.problemStatement:
This is a very easy warm-up problem.

You are given a string. Your task is to determine whether number of occurrences of some character in the string is equal to the sum of  the numbers of occurrences of other characters in the string.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single string S consisting only of lowercase Latin letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print YES if there exists a character whose number of occurrences equals the sum of occurrences of all other characters, otherwise print NO.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each string we check if some character occurs exactly half of the total length. 'acab' (len=4) has 'a' twice → YES. 'zzqzqq' (len=6) has 'z' three times → YES. 'abc' (len=3) odd length → NO. 'kklkwwww' (len=8) has 'w' four times → YES.
'aa' length 2, 'a' appears 2 times (needs 1) → NO. 'ab' length 2, each character appears once → YES. 'abcabc' length 6, each appears twice, need 3 → NO.
'zzzzzz' length 6, 'z' appears 6 times (needs 3) → NO. 'abcd' length 4, each appears once (needs 2) → NO.

solution.approach: The key insight is to calculate the frequency of each character in the string and then check if any character's frequency is equal to the sum of frequencies of all other characters. This can be achieved by iterating over the string to count the frequency of each character and then checking each character's frequency against the sum of all other frequencies.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * |S|) per test case, where T is the number of test cases and |S| is the length of the string. The total time complexity is O(T * |S|) since we process each test case independently.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) for the character frequency array, assuming the size of the alphabet is constant (26 for lowercase English letters).
